q: Environmental pollution is caused by the harmful ___ of substances into the environment.
Heavy metals are metals with high ___ and toxic effects even at very low concentrations.
Arsenic is rarely found in nature and is not very toxic, but it can be oxidised easily in humid air and becomes toxic by conversion to ___.
The desorption and dissolution of arsenic from arsenic rich ___ and minerals are the main sources of groundwater contamination and soil.
Sodium arsenate is used to kill some ___ in fruit cultivation and potato growing.
Cadmium is released into the atmosphere predominantly as elemental cadmium and cadmium oxide from some sources as ___.
___ is also a significant source of cadmium exposure.
Cadmium in the environment is also toxic to plants, animals and ___.
___ enters the atmosphere largely in the exhaust fumes from internal combustion engines.
The primary use of lead is in the manufacture of ___.
Elemental mercury occurs in ___.
Inorganic mercury can be transformed into ___ by microorganisms such as phytoplanktons and fungi.
The source of exposure to low levels is mercury released from ___ from the panning of gold and fillings in teeth.
Mercury is generally taken into the body in the form of ___.
A ___ is a substance that kills a pest, or it prevents or reduces the damage a pest may cause.
Pesticides are often grouped into "___" because they act on the pest in the same way.
___ occurs because of the attraction between a chemical and soil particles.
___ is the process of pesticide breakdown after application.
___ is a process by which substances are broken down by the action of sunlight.
POPs are also carbon-based organic compounds that have the special abilities to be ___.
POPs are chemically stable, remain intact in the environment for long periods, and are ___ in nature.
VOCs are organic chemicals that pollute the air and be absorbed or inhaled by humans, animals and ___.
VOCs play a significant role in the formation of ___ and fine particulates in the atmosphere.
The accumulation of ozone, fine particulates and other gaseous pollutants results in ___ that reduces visibility.
The burning of plastics releases toxic gases like ___, furans, mercury and polychlorinated biphenyls into the atmosphere.
